name: details: date-shaped but unparseable input re-prompts with NO writes — never stores a garbage date (B7/F15)
comment: '32 June' passes the date classifier (it contains 'jun') but is not a real date. Pre-B7 it was stored verbatim in Notes and the booking was created; B7 must re-prompt instead, because a booking whose structured dates are absent or wrong is invisible to — or poisons — B8's overlap check. This is the 'don't silently store garbage' rule, and it is the reason the parse happens before any write: the write count here must be zero, exactly as in fixture 04.

includes: Sorry, I didn't quite get that
